what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser deep enough into the DTD entity/element-decl path that xmlHashLookup3 is reached with a dict-interned name3 equal (by pointer) to a stored entry's name3; reaching it at all needs a large, well-formed DOCTYPE structure that survives many parse stages.
why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived energy gives faster/smaller/more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the FIFO corpus of structured DTD seeds is mutated enough to assemble the deep DOCTYPE that reaches and resolves the lookup (resolved 9/10, hitcount 36).
why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, so it never accumulates the mutation budget on the structured seeds and stays blocked 10/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage feeds a perf_score-derived per-seed mutation budget (StdPowerMutationalStage) that naive lacks; this calibrated energy is the single-technique delta enabling the winner.

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement; no comparison-solving aid, so the win comes from spending more mutation energy on the small well-formed XML seeds that already carry most of the prolog/markup.
why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived power schedule gives faster, smaller, more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the compact valid-XML seeds (e.g. the 257-258 byte ones) get heavily mutated and complete the entity-ref path, resolving 8/10.
why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc/token stack under a plain mutational stage with no energy weighting, spreading mutations uniformly and leaving the structural tokens unrepaired; it stays blocked 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer's calibrated energy (StdPowerMutationalStage with calibration-based perf_score, PowerSchedule None) concentrates havoc effort on small/stable well-formed seeds, raising the chance of completing the valid-XML structure without any CMP introspection.

what_input_feature: A byte equal to '-' (0x2D), '.' (0x2E), or 0xB7 positioned where xmlIsDocNameChar evaluates it as a name character (the parsed DOCTYPE/attribute-value name region around offsets 0x06-0x0c), so line 3556's true branch is taken instead of falling through.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds carry concentrated non-alnum name bytes in the early region (e.g. cb/cc runs and 0x32 fills at offsets 0x06-0x0c) and drive the branch's true side 84 times; the minimizer's calibrated power schedule grants more havoc mutations to these small/fast/stable seeds, so it explores enough byte permutations to land a '-'/'.'/0xB7 name char.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds hold a fixed '127772.xml' name string (bytes 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c at offsets 0x06-0x0d) whose chars all resolve via the earlier alnum/'.'-handled checks, never forcing the 0xB7 / '-' name-char path; with naive's flat per-seed energy the branch's true side is hit only 8 times and stays blocked 8/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: minimizer differs from naive only by a CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage that derives per-seed havoc count (energy) from a calibration perf_score; faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more mutations, giving minimizer more attempts to mutate a name byte into the '-'/'.'/0xB7 class that flips line 3556. This is calibrated-energy mutation budgeting, not comparison-solving.
